title: Approve/Deny UI with cascading impact preview
description: One-click approve and deny buttons per passenger. BEFORE approving: show cascading impact ('Approving seat 14A for Passenger X means Passenger Y first choice can no longer be fulfilled'). Deny with reason (dropdown or free text). On approve: visual confirmation. On deny: passenger gets notified automatically. The core decision-making interface.

title: Passenger wish stream & priority queue
description: Real-time list of passengers and their chosen preferences. Sorted by: priority (denied passengers first) then timestamp. Each entry shows: passenger name, original itinerary, chosen option, denial count. Live updates via WebSocket/SSE. Visual priority indicators (color coding for denial count). Filterable/searchable.

title: At-a-glance disruption overview
description: Top-level view showing disruption impact: how many passengers affected, percentage who will miss connections, disruption type and cause. Gate agent can see the big picture before diving into individual passengers. Example: '80% of passengers on LH456 will miss their connection'.

title: Status updates & confirmation flow
description: After submitting preference: show waiting state ('Your preference has been submitted to the gate agent'). On approval: show confirmation with clear next steps. On denial: show reason, present new options, allow re-selection. Real-time updates via WebSocket/SSE. Handle priority escalation messaging.

title: Dashboard — project setup & layout shell
description: Set up Next.js project for the gate agent dashboard. Desktop-optimized layout. Create shell with header (flight info, disruption summary), main content area (passenger queue), and sidebar (detail panel). Professional airline ops aesthetic.

title: Options selection & ranked preference UI
description: Display 3-4 concrete options with details: alternative flights (with seat info), hotel + next-day (with hotel name/location), ground transport (with route/times), alternative airport routing. Passenger can select single preference or rank multiple. Submit preference as hint to backend. Show option details clearly (times, availability, maps).

title: Approve/Deny workflow with cascading impact
description: When gate agent approves a passenger's wish: lock the resource (seat, hotel room), notify passenger of confirmation with next steps. When denying: generate reason, notify passenger, present new options, bump priority. Calculate and expose cascading impact before approval (which other passengers are affected).

title: Disruption notification & explanation view
description: Display disruption notification with plain-language explanation of WHY it happened. Show affected flight details (flight number, route, time). Show default auto-rebook if it exists ('The airline has already rebooked you onto LH1238'). Clear, empathetic copy. Receives data via WebSocket/SSE from backend.

title: State Manager — wishes, priorities, cascading
description: Manages passenger state: current wish/preference, approval status, denial count, priority level. Implements priority escalation: 1 denial → above first-choice passengers, 2 denials → highest priority. Handles cascading impact: when approving option X for passenger A, determine which other passengers lose their preferred option. Expose state via API.

title: Passenger App — project setup & layout shell
description: Set up Next.js project for the passenger app. Responsive web design (mobile-first). Create layout shell with navigation, loading states, and basic routing. Should look like a native airline app notification experience.

title: Google Grounding integration (Search + Maps)
description: Set up Gemini API with Google Search grounding and Google Maps grounding. Search grounding for: weather, NOTAMs, airport status, disruption context. Maps grounding for: ground transport routes, hotel proximity, airport distances. Wrap as reusable tools for the agent.

title: Option Generator — build passenger alternatives
description: Given a disrupted passenger, generate 3-4 concrete options: (1) Rebook to alternative flight (LH Schedules + Seat Maps), (2) Hotel + next-day flight (Gemini + Google Maps), (3) Ground transport (Gemini + Google Maps), (4) Alternative airport routing. Each option includes concrete details (times, seats, addresses). Uses Google Grounding tools.

title: LH Flight Ops API integration
description: Integrate Lufthansa Flight Ops API. Set up MQTT subscription for disruption events (cancellation, diversion, delay). Integrate Schedules API for alternative flights, Seat Maps API for availability, Reference Data API for airport data. Rate limit: 200 req/s.

title: Disruption Engine — event detection & classification
description: Core engine that receives MQTT disruption events from LH Flight Ops API and classifies them: cancellation, diversion, delay, gate change. Determines affected passengers per event. Triggers option generation pipeline. Should handle both single-flight and hub-wide disruptions (Munich snowstorm scenario).

title: Set up real-time communication (WebSocket/SSE)
description: Implement real-time push from backend to frontends. Passenger app needs: disruption notifications, option updates, approval/denial status. Gate agent dashboard needs: live passenger wish stream, priority queue updates. Evaluate WebSocket vs SSE.

title: Define API contracts (OpenAPI/TypeSpec)
description: Define shared API contracts between backend and frontends. Endpoints for: disruption events, passenger options, preference submission, approve/deny actions, status updates. Choose OpenAPI or TypeSpec. Generate types for both Python and TypeScript consumers.

title: [EPIC] API & Data Layer
description: Shared API contracts and data models. WebSocket/SSE real-time communication between backend and frontends. Mock data for demo scenarios (aircraft malfunction diversion, Munich snowstorm hub disruption). LH Flight Ops API integration. Google Maps + Search grounding integration.
notes:
LH Flight Ops API rate limit: 200 req/s — confirmed reasonable for demo scenarios with hundreds of passengers.
Switched to no-db (JSONL) mode for git-trackable issues.

title: [EPIC] Gate Agent Dashboard
description: Web-based dashboard for gate agents. Real-time stream of passenger preferences sorted by priority→timestamp. One-click approve/deny with cascading impact visibility. Manual resolution tool for edge cases. Shows passenger name, original itinerary, chosen option, denial count. Next.js.

title: [EPIC] Passenger App
description: Web-based passenger-facing app. Shows disruption explanation (plain language), auto-rebook default, 3-4 concrete options (alt flights, hotel+next-day, ground transport, alt airport). Passengers submit ranked preferences. Receives status updates and confirmations. Responsive web (Next.js).

title: [EPIC] Backend / Agent Server
description: Core backend: Disruption Engine, Option Generator, State Manager. Connects to LH Flight Ops API (MQTT), Google Gemini with Grounding, generates passenger options, manages cascading state and priority escalation. Python + LangChain/LangGraph.

title: Ahead-of-flight briefing card UI
description: Passenger app card showing 'What to expect today' before departure. Displays: weather, events impacting trip, transit info, airport alerts. Digestible format, not overwhelming. Should feel like a helpful travel companion. Integrates into passenger app's pre-flight view.

title: Ahead-of-flight context engine (Gemini grounding)
description: Backend service that queries Gemini with Google Search + Maps grounding X hours before departure. Gathers: weather at destination, local events/strikes, transit disruptions, NOTAMs, airport status. Returns structured context data per flight/passenger. Triggered by schedule proximity.

title: Presentation flow & demo script
description: Create presentation narrative tying the demo together. Story arc: problem (60B+ annual cost, 200M affected passengers) → solution (ReRoute) → live demo → impact. Talking points per screen. Backup plan if live demo fails (screenshots/video). Jury-facing materials highlighting Gemini grounding + LH API usage.

title: Manual resolution / edge case tool
description: For edge cases the AI cannot resolve. When passenger comes to desk, agent sees their full profile: original itinerary, all wishes submitted, denial history, current options. No digging through individual records — everything prepared. Free-text resolution notes. Override capability for special situations.

title: Demo Scenario 2: Snowstorm Munich → hub disruption
description: End-to-end demo of hub disruption. No flights leaving Munich. Show different handling by passenger type: destination=Munich (no action), connecting through Munich (full option set). Demonstrate cascading impact when gate agent approves one passenger affecting another. Show priority escalation after denial.

title: Demo Scenario 1: Aircraft malfunction → diversion
description: End-to-end demo of diversion scenario. Real existing flight, simulate aircraft malfunction. Passengers on board receive options via app. Demo walk-through: passenger choosing between reroute/hotel/ground transport, gate agent approving. Seeded data, scripted flow, reliable execution.

title: Denial & re-selection flow
description: When a passenger's preference is denied: display reason ('Unfortunately, your preferred option is no longer available because [reason]'). Present updated options (some may have changed). Allow new preference submission. Show denial count context if relevant. Smooth UX — should feel helpful, not frustrating.

title: Plain-language disruption explanation generator
description: Use Gemini with Google Search grounding to generate human-readable explanations of WHY a disruption happened. Input: disruption event data. Output: compassionate, clear explanation (e.g. 'Due to heavy snowfall in Munich, all departures are suspended until further notice. Here is what this means for you.'). Goal: create understanding so passengers don't need to ask the gate agent.

title: Mock data & demo scenario seeding
description: Create mock datasets for demo scenarios: Scenario 1 (aircraft malfunction diversion) and Scenario 2 (Munich snowstorm hub disruption). Include: passenger manifests, flight schedules, seat maps, disruption events. Seed data must be reproducible for demo reliability.

title: [EPIC] Ahead-of-Flight Intelligence
description:
Proactive passenger briefing before flight. Uses Google Grounding (Search + Maps) to surface events impacting the trip: weather at destination, local events/strikes, transit disruptions, NOTAMs, airport status. Pushed to passenger app ahead of departure as a 'What to expect today' card. Key differentiator: passengers are informed BEFORE things go wrong, not just after.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Gemini with Google Search grounding queries relevant context for upcoming flight
- Gemini with Google Maps grounding provides destination/transit context
- Results formatted as digestible cards in passenger app
- Triggered X hours before departure

title: [EPIC] Demo & Presentation
description: Demo scenario orchestration: Scenario 1 (aircraft malfunction → diversion), Scenario 2 (Munich snowstorm → hub disruption). Stretch: passenger-triggered incident (overslept). Demo data seeding, presentation flow, live demo reliability.
